data.narrative:
Nominal is a Series B AI & Software company founded by Cameron McCord, Bryce Strauss, and Jason Hoch, currently valued at $1.0B post-money with $155M raised to date. The applicable peer universe comprises 4 same-stage, same-sector private companies (Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, Photoroom) plus 1 public comparable (Palantir), providing meaningful cross-validation across early-stage frontier AI valuations.

Two valuation methods triangulate a defensible range. The Stage-Progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implying $1.9B. The Peer-Median method references the four comparable Series B AI companies, which exhibit a median valuation of $5.3B, though with material dispersion: range of $500M to $32.0B. The peer-median approach acknowledges that frontier AI valuations remain volatile and stage-independent; Safe Superintelligence at $32.0B and Photoroom at $500M demonstrate sector heterogeneity.

These methods converge on a triangulated range of $1.6B (low) to $6.1B (high), with a median of $3.6B. This range reflects meaningful tension between internally-consistent stage progression and observable peer valuations that span an order of magnitude. Key uncertainties include definitional variance in what constitutes "frontier tech" within Series B cohorts, the durability of outlier valuations (particularly Safe Superintelligence), and limited public-market data points for calibration. The current $1.0B valuation sits below the implied range, suggesting potential upside if performance tracks peer benchmarks.
